mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7.5% signals labor market slack and reduced wage pressure, which decreases corporate cost inflation expectations and supports equity valuations. This mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon as labor market signals propagate through earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 1.5%, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 101.0 (index 2017=100) combined with stable or declining inflation signals productive capacity expansion without cost-push pressure, supporting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction signal (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL momentum) falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following trigger activation are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 141,500 thousand persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which increases consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS level and 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are not significantly higher during periods of PAYEMS > 141500 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units signals reduced construction demand and future residential investment weakness, which dampens aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1416.0 thousand units near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC between HOUST and 20-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4%) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) indicates monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces present value of future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% well below tightening threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction signal (CPIAUCSL momentum × FEDFUNDS level) falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 17,200 billion chained 2017 dollars signals economic deceleration and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. When combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 7.5%), this dual weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth recession fears intensify.
regime_hint: growth recession or post-shock recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction signal (GDPC1 level × UNRATE level) falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following trigger activation are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
